note: All generator IDs are pool-isolated. No shared IDs with other pools.

generators.name: Quadratic with Complex Solutions (Square Root Method)

params.note: Generate from roots r1, r2 so c=r1*r2, b=r1+r2

contexts: A rectangle has width w. Its length is {a} more than twice its width. The area is {b} sq ft. Write an equation for w.
A ball is thrown upward. Its height is h = -{a}t² + {b}t + {c}. When does it reach height {d}?
The sum of a number and {a} times its square is {b}. Write the equation.

params.template: Solve and check for extraneous solutions: √({a}x + {b}) = x + {c}
Find the average rate of change of f(x) = {a}x² + {b}x + {c} from x = {x1} to x = {x2}
Translate point ({x}, {y}) by vector <{dx}, {dy}>. What are the new coordinates?
Reflect point ({x}, {y}) across the {line}. What are the new coordinates?
Rotate point ({x}, {y}) by {angle}° counterclockwise about the origin.
Point P({x},{y}) is first reflected across {line1}, then rotated {angle}° about origin. Find P''.
Dilate point ({x},{y}) by scale factor {k} about the origin.

generators.name: Find Missing Side via Similar Triangles

params.template: Triangles ABC ~ DEF. AB={a}, BC={b}, DE={c}. Find EF.
In right △, altitude to hypotenuse creates segments {p} and {q}. Find the altitude.
In right △ABC with right angle at C, if the legs are {a} and {b}, find sin(A), cos(A), tan(A).
In a right triangle, angle A = {angle}°, hypotenuse = {hyp}. Find the opposite side.
In right △, opposite = {opp}, hypotenuse = {hyp}. Find angle A (nearest degree).
Find all missing sides and angles in right △ABC (right angle at C). Given: {given}.
Central angle AOB = {angle}°. Find arc AB.
Inscribed angle = {angle}°. Find the intercepted arc.
Find the arc length for a circle with radius {r} and central angle {theta}°.
Find the area of a sector with radius {r} and central angle {theta}°.
Write the equation of a circle with center ({h},{k}) and radius {r}.
Find center and radius: x² + y² + {a}x + {b}y + {c} = 0
params.note: Must complete the square for both x and y

params.template: Use the table to find P({event_a} | {event_b})
P(A) = {pa}, P(B) = {pb}. If A and B are independent, find P(A and B).
A bag has {r} red and {b} blue marbles. Draw 2 without replacement. Find P(red, red).
P(A) = {pa}, P(B) = {pb}. A and B are mutually exclusive. Find P(A or B).

contexts: A restaurant has {a} entrees, {b} sides, and {c} drinks. How many different meals?
A PIN has {n} digits (0-9). How many different PINs?
You have {a} shirts, {b} pants, {c} shoes. How many outfits?

params.template: How many ways can {r} items be arranged from {n} distinct items?
In how many ways can you choose {r} items from {n} distinct items?

contexts: A committee of {r} is chosen from {n} people. Find P(specific person is included).
A lottery picks {r} numbers from 1–{n}. Find P(picking {r} specific numbers).

params.template: f(x) = {a}(x-{h})² + {k}. Identify vertex, axis, min/max, and zeros.
Solve graphically: y = x² + {b}x + {c} and y = {m}x + {k}
Find the x-value(s) where f(x) = g(x). f(x) = {f}, g(x) = {g}
Two chords intersect inside a circle, creating arcs {arc1}° and {arc2}°. Find the angle.
Two chords intersect at P. AX={a}, XB={b}, CX={c}. Find XD.

contexts: In how many ways can {n} people be arranged in a row?
How many 3-digit numbers can be formed from digits {set} with no repetition?
How many ways can {n} books be arranged on a shelf?
